name: Monkey Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in warm milk in a small bowl.
2. In a large bowl, combine the yeast mixture, egg, oil, flour, sugar, and salt. Beat until smooth.
3. Very slowly drizzle in boiling water while beating continuously.
4. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
5. The next day, coat a 10-inch bundt pan or tube pan with nonstick cooking spray and set aside.
6. Punch down the dough, turn onto a well-floured pastry cloth, and knead for 1 minute.
7. With a floured rolling pin, roll the dough into a rectangle that is 1/4 inch thick.
8. Cut into strips that are 3-1/2 to 4 inches long and 1-1/2 to 2 inches wide.
9. Dip the strips in melted butter, then arrange them, overlapping, in the pan.
10. Cover with the cloth and let rise in a warm, dry spot away from drafts for 1-1/2 hours until doubled in bulk.
11. Toward the end of rising, preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
12. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until golden brown and the loaf sounds hollow when tapped.
13. Cool the loaf in the pan on a wire rack for 5 minutes before turning it out onto the rack and serving.
